rir.title: Reps in Reserve
rir.description: How many more reps could you have done?
rir.infoText: RIR measures how many reps you had left before failure. It helps the app suggest weight adjustments.

cycleSummary.subtitle: Here's how you did
preSession.scopeDescription: Just this session only changes your next run. Apply permanently updates this workout day for all future sessions. Past workouts stay logged under the exercises you used.
preSession.scopeDescriptionInSession: Just this session applies only to the current workout. Apply permanently updates this workout day for all future sessions.
preSession.scopeSwapHistoryHint: Past sessions for this day stay under the previous exercise in history and progression charts.
preSession.startBlocked: Each exercise needs at least one set before you can start.
preSession.mutationError: Could not save. Check your connection and try again.
preSession.deleteLoggedDescription: You have logged sets for this exercise. Deleting it will remove these records from this session.

historySheet.durationInfoBody: This line shows your best hold time per workout — the longest duration you logged for a set in that session.

progression.holdIncompleteDetailDuration: Some sets missed target hold last time. Keeping {{sets}}×{{duration}}s @ {{weight}} {{unit}}.
progression.holdNearFailureDetail: Average RIR was very low last time. Staying at {{sets}}×{{reps}} @ {{weight}} {{unit}} until you have more in the tank.
progression.holdNearFailureDetailDuratio: Average RIR was very low last time. Staying at {{sets}}×{{duration}}s @ {{weight}} {{unit}} until you have more in the tank.

progression.weightUpDetailDuration: Duration ceiling hit — weight bumped to {{weight}} {{unit}}. {{sets}}×{{duration}}s.

progression.setsUpDetailDuration: Weight maxed out — added a set: {{sets}}×{{duration}}s @ {{weight}} {{unit}}.

progression.plateauDetailDuration: All dimensions maxed at {{sets}}×{{duration}}s @ {{weight}} {{unit}}. Time for a new stimulus!

progression.tryGenerator: Try the AI program generator for a new cycle
autoDetectLoading: {{name}}: you logged weight — unlock weight progression?
historySheet.trendHintBodyweight: Log this exercise on more days to see your best reps trend.
historySheet.chartAriaReps: Reps trend from {{min}} to {{max}} across recent sessions
historySheet.repsInfoBody:
This line shows your best rep count per workout — the highest number of reps you logged in a single set during that session.

For bodyweight exercises, tracking max reps is a more meaningful progress indicator than estimated 1RM.
historySheet.epleyInfoBody:
This line shows your best estimated one-rep max per workout — the weight you could likely lift for a single rep, inferred from what you actually logged (weight × reps).

We use the Epley formula, a common strength-training shortcut. It’s not lab-accurate, but it helps compare sessions when you lift heavier or use fewer reps.
